note: Language Switcher Dropdown renders core's language switcher as a <select> dropdown. It has no settings page; everything is configured on a placed `block` config entity using the language_dropdown_block plugin (derived per language type, e.g. language_dropdown_block:language_interface). Grounded in the 2.4.x docs (data.json, usage.md, agent/). Cases are tiered: easy=recipe (response-graded), medium=introspection (setup a known block config entity, agent inspects live via drush, cleanup after), hard=execution (agent places a block config entity, verified against live site state).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Language Switcher Dropdown (lang_dropdown) module enabled, what block do I place to show the language dropdown, and what is its block plugin id? Is there a separate settings page?
evals.expected_output: Place the 'Language dropdown switcher' block via core's Block layout (Structure > Block layout; the module's configure route is block.admin_display). Its plugin id is language_dropdown_block, derived per language type (e.g. language_dropdown_block:language_interface). There is no dedicated settings form — you configure it in the block form.

evals.prompt: In the lang_dropdown block settings, which key selects how the dropdown is rendered (the output type / widget style) and what are its allowed integer values?
evals.expected_output: The `widget` setting picks the output type: 0 = Simple HTML select (no JS library), 1 = Marghoob Suleman msDropdown, 2 = Chosen, 3 = ddSlick.

evals.prompt: The lang_dropdown block has a `display` setting for the language label format. What do its integer values mean, and what does the `showall` setting do?
evals.expected_output: `display` chooses the label format: 0 = translated into current language, 1 = language native name, 2 = language code, 3 = translated into target language. `showall` (boolean) lists every enabled language even when the current page has no translation for it (those redirect to the home page).

evals.prompt: A Language Switcher Dropdown block with machine name `eval_ld_known` is already placed on this Drupal site (drush available). Inspect the running site and tell me which output type / widget style it is configured to use.
evals.expected_output: The block.block.eval_ld_known config entity has settings.widget = 2, i.e. the Chosen output type.

evals.prompt: A Language Switcher Dropdown block with machine name `eval_ld_known` is already placed on this Drupal site (drush available). Inspect the running site and tell me which language label format (the `display` setting) it uses and what its dropdown width is.
evals.expected_output: The block.block.eval_ld_known config entity has settings.display = 2 (Language Code label format) and settings.width = 250.

evals.prompt: A Language Switcher Dropdown block with machine name `eval_ld_known` is already placed on this Drupal site (drush available). Inspect it and tell me: is it configured to show all enabled languages even without a translation (the `showall` option)?
evals.expected_output: Yes — block.block.eval_ld_known has settings.showall = true, so it lists every enabled language regardless of whether the current page has a translation.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), place a Language Switcher Dropdown block (module lang_dropdown) with machine name `eval_ld_place` in the default theme. Configure it to use the ddSlick output type (widget style), the 'Language Native Name' display format, and a dropdown width of 180 pixels. Apply it to the live site so the block config entity really exists; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A block config entity `eval_ld_place` using a language_dropdown_block plugin exists with settings widget=3 (ddSlick), display=1 (native name), width=180. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), place a Language Switcher Dropdown block (module lang_dropdown) with machine name `eval_ld_simple` in the default theme. Configure it to use the Simple HTML select output type (no JS library), the 'Language Code' display format, and a dropdown width of 220 pixels. Apply it to the live site so the block config entity really exists; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A block config entity `eval_ld_simple` using a language_dropdown_block plugin exists with settings widget=0 (Simple HTML select), display=2 (language code), width=220. Verified against live site state.
